title: 7 Herbal Creams For Fungal Skin Infection
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great way to treat fungal skin infections like athlete's foot or ringworm. These creams are made from natural herbs that have antifungal properties, which help to kill the fungus and soothe the affected skin. One example is Aloe vera, which is known for its cooling and anti-inflammatory properties. It can help to calm redness and itching, making it a great addition to any antifungal treatment.  Another example is Eucalyptus globulus, which has natural decongestant properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. This herb is often used in creams and ointments to help clear up fungal infections quickly.  Calendula officinalis is another herb that's commonly used in herbal creams for fungal skin infections. It has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent infection and reduce redness and swelling.  Using herbal creams can bring several benefits to your life. For one, they're often gentler on the skin than prescription medications, which can be harsh and dry out the skin. They're also a more natural and holistic approach to treating fungal infections, which can be appealing to people who prefer to avoid chemicals. Additionally, herbal creams can be used in conjunction with other treatments, like tea tree oil or probiotics, to create a comprehensive treatment plan.  Herbal creams are also often easy to make at home, using simple ingredients like coconut oil, beeswax, and dried herbs. This can be a fun and rewarding experience, especially for people who enjoy gardening or cooking. And, of course, using natural products can be a great way to take care of your skin and overall health, without relying on harsh chemicals or medications.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for fungal skin infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contains active constituents like aloe-emodin, aloin, and anthraquinones. These compounds have antifungal properties that help fight fungal infections. Aloe vera also contains salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling caused by fungal infections. The antimicrobial properties of aloe vera help prevent the spread of fungal infections by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate the condition. The combination of antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties in aloe vera creams makes it a potential treatment option for fungal skin infections.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for fungal skin infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 1 tablespoon of tea tree oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
In a small bowl, mix 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil until smooth.
Add shea butter, tea tree oil, and vitamin E oil to the aloe vera mixture. Mix until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Apply a thin layer to affected skin areas 2-3 times a day, or as needed, to help soothe and treat fungal skin infections.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains bioactive constituents such as cineole, a compound that has antifungal properties. Cineole helps to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing the severity of fungal skin infections. Eucalyptus globulus also contains other compounds like globulol and viridiflorol, which have antimicrobial properties that help to combat fungal infections. The antifungal and antimicrobial properties of Eucalyptus globulus creams make it a good treatment option for fungal skin infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm. By reducing fungal growth and inflammation, Eucalyptus globulus creams can help to promote healing and prevent the spread of infection.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for fungal skin infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus globulus essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of tea tree oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated. Continue heating until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add eucalyptus globulus essential oil and tea tree oil, and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before using. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoids, flavonoids, and carotenoids as active constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help fight fungal skin infections. The triterpenoids, such as oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, inhibit the growth of fungi, while the flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, reduce inflammation and promote healing. The carotenoids, like beta-carotene and lutein, have antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. By combining these properties, Calendula officinalis creams help to control fungal skin infections and promote healthy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for fungal skin infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the coconut oil in the saucepan over low heat. Add the dried Calendula flowers and let it simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Discard the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan. Heat until melted.
Combine the Calendula-infused oil with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture. Stir well and pour into jars. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have antifungal properties that help combat fungal skin infections by inhibiting the growth of fungi and reducing inflammation. Alkylamides, in particular, have been shown to have a direct effect on fungal cells, disrupting their cell membranes and ultimately leading to their death. Caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid have antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by the infection. By applying Echinacea purpurea creams directly to the affected area, the antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties can help to clear up the infection and promote healthy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for fungal skin infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted oil and beeswax mixture. Let it steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Add the shea butter and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir until it's well combined. Pour the mixture into a tin or a jar and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate. These constituents have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to reduce fungal growth and fight off infections. The antiseptic properties of linalool also aid in preventing the spread of fungal infections by killing bacteria that can cause secondary infections. Linalyl acetate has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. By targeting fungal growth and inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia creams can provide relief and support in the treatment of fungal skin infections.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for fungal skin infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Remove from heat and stir in shea butter until it melts.
Add lavender essential oil and mix well. Then, slowly add distilled water while stirring continuously.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify before applying to the affected skin area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains active constituents like hyperforin and hypericin. These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help combat fungal skin infections by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells. Hyperforin, in particular, has been shown to disrupt the cell membrane of fungi, ultimately leading to their death. The antifungal properties of hypericin also contribute to its effectiveness in treating fungal skin infections. By targeting the underlying cause of the infection, Hypericum perforatum creams can help alleviate symptoms and promote healing in affected areas.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for fungal skin infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Add the dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Then, add the beeswax and stir until it's melted.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once cooled, use a spoon to scoop out the cream as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains the active constituents cineole and terpinen-4-ol. Cineole has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of fungi on the skin. Terpinen-4-ol has antifungal properties that kill fungi and prevent new infections. These compounds also help reduce inflammation and promote healing of infected skin. By killing fungi and reducing inflammation, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help clear up fungal skin infections.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for fungal skin infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Distilled Water, 1/4 cup of Carrier Oil (Coconut or Olive Oil), 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia Essential Oil, and a clean mixing bowl.
In the mixing bowl, combine the Distilled Water and Carrier Oil. Stir until well mixed.
Add the Melaleuca alternifolia Essential Oil to the water-oil mixture. Stir gently to avoid creating bubbles.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar. Stir occasionally as the mixture cools to room temperature.
Once cooled, use the mixture to make creams by mixing it with a Cream Base (available in cosmetic stores or online). Apply the cream to affected skin areas 2-3 times a day.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent fungal skin infections. Some herbs like oregano and tea tree have antifungal properties that can fight off fungus. These teas can be consumed as a preventive measure, especially for people prone to fungal skin infections. However, it's best to consume them regularly to see any benefits.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be helpful for soothing fungal skin infections, it's not recommended to consume them daily. Some teas contain ingredients like echinacea and goldenseal, which can be too potent for regular use. This might cause interactions with any medications you're taking or lead to side effects like digestive issues or allergic reactions.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with fungal skin infections, but it takes time to see results. Some people notice improvements within a week or two, while others may need a few weeks or even months. It's a gradual process, and consistent use is key. Be patient and give it time to work.
faq_time: For fungal skin infections like athlete's foot, drinking herbal tea in the morning may help. The antifungal properties in the tea can start fighting the infection early in the day. Drinking tea in the morning also gives the active ingredients time to work throughout the day, potentially reducing symptoms.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for fungal skin infections. For example, if you're taking ketoconazole for a fungal infection, drinking St. John's Wort tea might not be a good idea. St. John's Wort can make ketoconazole less effective. This is because St. John's Wort affects the way your body processes certain medications.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and have a fungal skin infection, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like those containing sage, pennyroyal, and evening primrose, can stimulate the uterus or affect blood pressure, which may not be safe for you and your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have a fungal skin infection. Some herbal teas, like those with antifungal properties, might be helpful, but others could affect your milk supply or your baby's sensitivity. It's recommended to check the ingredients and choose teas from trusted sources.
title_2: Fungal Skin Infection in Runners: 7 Effective Herbal Creams
